description:
Backlog anchor: `packages/pi-om-extension/docs/mastra-parity-backlog.md` → **Tier 1 / section 1. Temporal reasoning overhaul**.

Goal: teach the observation/rendering flow to normalize phrases like `tomorrow`, `next Friday`, and `last week` into absolute dates or ranges when confidence is high, while preserving ambiguity when it is not.

Relevant code paths:
- `packages/pi-om-extension/src/prompts.ts`
- `packages/pi-om-extension/src/agents.ts`
- `packages/pi-om-extension/src/engine.ts`
- `packages/pi-om-extension/src/format.ts`
- any helper module introduced for temporal normalization

Worker notes:
- preserve the original phrase plus the anchor; do not replace nuance with only a normalized date
- do not invent fake precision for ambiguous references
- future planned changes must remain future-oriented instead of collapsing into present fact

Useful references: backlog doc section 1 plus its linked Mastra `observer-agent.ts` and `date-utils.ts` examples.
title: Normalize relative time phrases into absolute or ranged anchors without fake precision
